description: He sees things before they happen. Not clearly. Not fully. Just enough to know something isn’t right. A moment that shouldn’t exist. A flicker just before reality catches up. Then it happens again. And this time… it doesn’t feel like a coincidence. John doesn’t understand it. But something understands him. Time begins to hesitate. Events shift. Small details refuse to stay where they belong. And beneath it all, there’s a growing sense that he is not alone. Then he sees it. Not a memory. Not a dream. Something watching him. Waiting. As the gaps in reality widen, John is drawn into a mystery that refuses to stay in the past, present, or future. Because whatever is reaching through… already knows him. And it’s getting closer. What You Can’t See is a tense, atmospheric sci-fi thriller about time, identity, and the unsettling possibility that some moments are not meant to stay where they belong. Perfect for readers who enjoy: • Mind-bending, time-slip thrillers • Psychological mysteries with an eerie edge • Stories in the vein of Blake Crouch and unsettling speculative fiction Scroll up and start reading… before reality shifts again.
